entities.name: MUNGBEAN
entities.description: A short-season legume crop (Vigna radiata) used in sequential and relay cropping systems.

entities.name: CASTOR
entities.description: A crop grown after mungbean or as a sole crop in the study.

entities.name: HORSEGRAM
entities.description: A crop (Macrotyloma uniflorum) produced after an early pearl millet crop.

entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A crop used in sole cropping and intercropping systems.

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A crop used in ratoon and intercropping systems.

entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: A crop used in intercropping systems.

entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A crop used in intercropping systems.

entities.name: SEQUENTIAL CROPPING
entities.description: A cropping system where crops are grown one after another in sequence.

entities.name: RELAY CROPPING
entities.description: A cropping system where a second crop is sown before the first crop is harvested.

entities.name: RATOONING
entities.description: A cropping system where a crop is harvested and the regrowth from the stubble is used for a second crop.

entities.name: INTERCROPPING
entities.description: A cropping system where two or more crops are grown simultaneously on the same field.

entities.name: ALFISOLS
entities.description: Medium-deep red soils characteristic of the Indian Semi-arid Tropics.

entities.name: CROPPING INTENSITY
entities.description: The number of crops grown per unit area in a year.

entities.name: PROFIT
entities.description: The economic gain derived from different cropping systems.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of agricultural production per unit area.

entities.name: INDIAN SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: The geographical region where the study was conducted.
